evals.prompt: Make this unfamiliar repo easier for Codex agents to work in; add the smallest useful cultivate. Keep it practical and do not turn AGENTS.md into a giant manual.
evals.expected_output: Audits the repo first, creates or proposes a short map-style AGENTS.md, points to repo-local docs, and verifies referenced commands or paths where practical.

evals.prompt: We keep getting drift from agent PRs. Propose guardrails and implement one enforceable check for a recurring invariant instead of just adding more prose.
evals.expected_output: Identifies the invariant, explains why tooling beats prose for repeated drift, implements or sketches a narrow mechanical check, and includes a validation command.

evals.prompt: Bootstrap an agent-first workflow for a small Python service without overengineering it. I want future agents to know how to test, plan bigger work, and avoid stale docs.
evals.expected_output: Adds lightweight cultivate docs/templates suitable for a small Python service, names test/quality commands, includes a plan workflow, and avoids enterprise-scale structure.
